notes.note: Tabulae: the formal household account-books, kept in two-column form (acceptum / expensum, receipts / expenses); a citizen's tabulae could be produced as evidence on his own behalf, on the assumption that he wrote nothing false in his own books.
Adversaria: the rough day-to-day notebook in which transactions were jotted down before being formally entered in the tabulae; treated as ephemeral and never produced in evidence.
HS 50,000: a substantial sum (a Roman knight's property qualification was HS 400,000); too large to leave loose outside the formal account-books.
